# Workplan

<!-- Generated by ClaudeContextGenerator — review and adjust before creating YouTrack tickets -->

**Total estimated time:** 14.0d

## 1. Setup Project Structure and Development Environment

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 0.5d

Create the project directory structure following the specified architecture with proper separation of concerns. Set up pyproject.toml with all dependencies, configure development tools (black, ruff, mypy), and establish pre-commit hooks. Initialize git repository and create basic CI/CD configuration files.

## 2. Implement Core Configuration Data Models

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Develop Pydantic v2 models for all configuration entities including DeviceConfig, SonnenBatterieConfig, HomeWizardConfig, and SolarEdgeConfig. Implement comprehensive validation rules with custom validators for IP addresses, ports, and device-specific parameters. Create configuration loading and validation utilities with proper error handling.

## 3. Create Database Schema and Repository Pattern

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Design and implement SQLAlchemy 2.0 models for device readings, system state, and configuration history. Create Alembic migrations for initial schema. Implement repository pattern with async SQLAlchemy, including base repository class and specific repositories for each entity. Add unit of work pattern for transaction management.

## 4. Build Abstract Device Client Interface

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 4h

Create the abstract DeviceClient base class with standardized methods for device communication. Define DeviceReading dataclass and common error types. Implement factory pattern for device client instantiation and registry system for different device types.

## 5. Implement sonnenBatterie Integration Client

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Develop the sonnenBatterie client with support for both v1 and v2 APIs. Implement automatic API version detection, secure token management, and connection pooling. Add comprehensive error handling with circuit breaker pattern and retry logic. Include data validation and normalization for USOC and power readings.

## 6. Implement HomeWizard Device Integration

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Create HomeWizard device client with auto-discovery via mDNS/SSDP scanning. Implement device-specific logic for different HomeWizard devices (P1 meter, car charger, solar meter). Add calibration procedures and multi-device support with redundancy handling. Include real-time validation and threshold management.

## 7. Implement SolarEdge Modbus Control Client

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1.5d

Develop SolarEdge Modbus client with safety-first design including pre-flight checks and command verification. Implement gradual power ramping, status monitoring, and fault detection. Add comprehensive safety mechanisms including emergency stops and rate limiting. Create read-back confirmation system for all control commands.

## 8. Create ENTSO-E Price Data Integration

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Implement multi-source price data strategy with ENTSO-E Transparency Platform as primary source. Add secondary APIs and fallback mechanisms using local database with historical patterns. Create price data validation, interpolation for gaps, and short-term forecasting. Include CSV import functionality for testing scenarios.

## 9. Build Decision Engine and Rule System

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1.5d

Implement strategy pattern-based decision engine with pluggable rules for battery management and pricing strategies. Create rule validation system with dry-run capabilities. Add SoC threshold management with hysteresis, charging/discharging rate limits, and safety override controls. Include comprehensive decision logging and audit trails.

## 10. Develop FastAPI Web Interface and REST APIs

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1d

Create FastAPI application with RESTful endpoints for device status, configuration management, and system control. Implement dependency injection for service layers and add comprehensive API documentation with OpenAPI. Create health check endpoints and real-time WebSocket connections for live data streaming.

## 11. Create Configuration GUI Application

- [x] **Estimated time:** 2d (delivered as MVP — encryption / mDNS / dry-run sim deferred)

Build modern tabbed configuration interface using tkinter with ttk styling. Implement all four configuration tabs (Device Configuration, Decision Logic, System & Monitoring, Validation & Deployment) with real-time validation and testing capabilities. Add configuration import/export, versioning, and backup functionality with encryption support.

## 12. Implement Structured Logging and Monitoring System

- [x] **Estimated time:** 1d (delivered as MVP — log viewer / metrics / alerting deferred)

Set up structlog with comprehensive logging categories and levels. Create real-time log viewer with filtering, search, and export capabilities. Implement metrics collection for KPIs including response times, success rates, and system resources. Add dashboard integration with alerting and automated reporting.

## 13. Write Comprehensive Test Suite

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 1.5d

Develop unit tests achieving >90% code coverage using pytest and pytest-asyncio. Create integration tests for device communication and end-to-end workflows. Implement property-based testing with hypothesis for edge cases. Add performance tests for high-frequency operations and contract tests for API compatibility. Create test fixtures and mocks for all external dependencies.

## 14. Create Documentation and Deployment Guides

- [ ] **Estimated time:** 0.5d

Write comprehensive documentation using MkDocs with Material theme. Create installation guides, configuration tutorials, and operational procedures. Document all APIs with examples and create troubleshooting guides. Add deployment procedures with rollback capabilities and maintenance automation scripts.

## 15. Dashboard Day-Ahead Price Chart

- [x] **Estimated time:** 0.5d (delivered — bar + SoC line merged into one combined dual-axis chart; ON/OFF zone shading dropped at user request)

Render the day-ahead injection-price bar chart on the dashboard, per the project spec ("24-hour day-ahead injection price bar chart (today + tomorrow's not needed), current hour highlighted, negative-price hours coloured distinctly"). Add a vendored chart library (Chart.js or similar — no CDN, since the orchestrator runs on a home LAN), a JSON endpoint that returns today's + tomorrow's hourly prices from the orchestrator's price cache, and the dashboard wiring that renders the bars with the spec-mandated highlighting (current hour, negative-price hours). Also add the matching SoC + injection-price chart with ON/OFF zone shading from the same spec section. Depends on the price cache being populated by the orchestrator tick loop. The graph should also display the battery charge state over the course of the day. Use line graph

## 16. Web Config Editor (`/config` page)

- [x] **Estimated time:** 0.5d (delivered)

Add a `/config` page to the FastAPI dashboard so device IPs, ports, API keys, and decision thresholds can be edited from the browser instead of hand-editing `config.yaml`. Mirror the tkinter editor's grouping (Devices / Decision / System), reuse `gui.binding.form_to_config` for validation and `save_with_backup` for writes (single `.bak` rotation). Field-keyed Pydantic errors render inline next to each input; a successful save shows a banner reminding the user that a restart is required for changes to take effect. Out of scope: hot reload (next phase if iteration friction warrants it), authentication (personal-use localhost deployment only).

## 17. Embed Swagger UI in dashboard nav (`/docs`)

- [x] **Estimated time:** 0.1d (delivered)

The auto-generated FastAPI `/docs` page didn't carry the dashboard's navbar, so users hit the API tab and lost their way back. Disable the default via `docs_url=None`, render a custom `/docs` route that extends `base.html` and embeds `swagger-ui-bundle` with the same CDN URLs FastAPI defaults to. Same Swagger experience, framed by the dashboard nav. Vendoring swagger-ui assets under `static/vendor/` deferred until offline operation matters.

## 18. Live log viewer (`/logs` page)

- [x] **Estimated time:** 0.4d (delivered)

Add a `/logs` page between Config and API that streams `logs/energy_orchestrator.log` to the browser in real time. Server-Sent Events endpoint at `/api/logs/stream` opens the rotating JSON log file, seeks back ~16 KB on connect for context, follows new lines via `asyncio.to_thread(f.readline)` so file I/O doesn't block the event loop, detects rotation by comparing on-disk size to read position and reopens, bails on `request.is_disconnected()`. Browser-side viewer: level dropdown (debug+/info+/warning+/error+/critical), substring filter, follow toggle, pause/resume, clear, auto-trim to 1000 rows. No new deps.
